dataset: Ekylibre demo with GAEC JOULIN farm data (Charente-Maritime, France)

pi_items.source: Consistent across task.json (line 17), README.md (line 29), setup_task.sh (line 27), export_result.sh (line 27), and verifier.py (line 28). Format follows standard French credit note convention (AV = Avoir, 2025 = year, 004 = sequence number).

Stated in task.json (line 18) and README.md (line 28). Web search found other AgriMat companies but not 'AgriMat 17' specifically, indicating synthetic/fictional supplier name created for task scenario. README.md (line 13) provides context: 'A farmer at GAEC JOULIN returned unused fencing materials...to their local supplier AgriMat 17'.

Stated as €150.00 (pretax) in task.json (line 19), README.md (lines 17, 34). Referenced as '1 unit of Matériel de clôture at €150.00' (fencing material) in README.md (line 34). Amount is reasonable for returned fencing materials and consistent across all verification files.
Stated in task.json (line 20) and README.md (line 30). Format is valid ISO 8601 date. Year 2024 is consistent with task creation timeframe.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a supplier credit note in Ekylibre for GAEC JOULIN farm (real French farm from Charente-Maritime). The credit note must have reference number AV-2025-004, supplier AgriMat 17, pretax amount €150.00, and date 2024-11-15. The supplier AgriMat 17 and specific credit note are synthetic task requirements (not pre-existing in the GAEC JOULIN demo data). The database queries verify the credit note by reference number, supplier name, amount, document type (PurchaseCreditNote), and creation timestamp validation to prevent pre-existing data gaming.
